category_label: NOT a country that has won the World Cup
explanation: The Netherlands have lost 3 finals (1974, 1978, 2010) without winning.

category_label: NOT a player who won the 2014 World Cup
explanation: Messi lost the 2014 final to Germany.

category_label: NOT a player who won the 2022 World Cup
explanation: Mbappé scored a hat-trick in the final but France lost on penalties.

category_label: NOT a country that has hosted the World Cup
explanation: Portugal has reached WC semi-finals but never hosted the tournament.

category_label: NOT a goalkeeper
explanation: Ramos is a centre-back.

category_label: NOT a player from Brazil's 2002 World Cup winning squad
explanation: Neymar was 10 years old in 2002 and not in the squad.

category_label: NOT a country that has reached a World Cup final since 2000
explanation: Norway has not qualified for a World Cup since 1998.

category_label: NOT a player who has played at a World Cup
explanation: Haaland's Norway has not qualified for a World Cup during his career.

category_label: NOT a manager who has won the World Cup
explanation: Guardiola has won everything in club football but has never managed at a World Cup.

category_label: NOT a venue that has hosted a World Cup final
explanation: Camp Nou hosted a 1982 semi-final but never a final; Spain's 1982 final was at the Bernabéu.

category_label: NOT a player who has lifted the World Cup as captain
explanation: Ronaldo has captained Portugal at multiple World Cups but has never won.

category_label: NOT a French player who won the 2018 World Cup
explanation: Benzema was exiled from the French squad at the time and did not play.

category_label: NOT a player who scored in a World Cup final
explanation: England did not reach a World Cup final during Rooney's career.

category_label: NOT a 2022 World Cup semi-finalist
explanation: Spain were knocked out by Morocco in the round of 16; Morocco was the fourth semi-finalist.

category_label: NOT a player who won the World Cup Golden Boot since 2000
explanation: Lewandowski has scored at World Cups but never finished as the tournament top scorer.

category_label: NOT a country that has reached a World Cup semi-final this century
explanation: Mexico has reached the round of 16 in 7 straight WCs but has never gone further.

category_label: NOT a country that has finished as World Cup runner-up since 2000
explanation: Italy won 2006 and was last runner-up in 1994.

category_label: NOT a German player who scored in the 2014 World Cup knockout stages
explanation: Schweinsteiger played every knockout match but did not score in the tournament.

category_label: NOT a player who has appeared in 4 or more World Cups
explanation: Vinícius has appeared at one World Cup so far (2022).

category_label: NOT a city that has hosted a World Cup final since 2000
explanation: The 2002 final was at International Stadium Yokohama; Tokyo did not host any 2002 matches.

category_label: NOT a manager who has reached a World Cup final since 2000
explanation: Mourinho has never managed a national team at a World Cup.

category_label: NOT a city that hosted a 2018 World Cup match
explanation: The 11 host cities were all in European Russia; Vladivostok was not selected.

category_label: NOT a player who has won the World Cup Golden Glove since 2002
explanation: Lloris won the 2018 World Cup but Thibaut Courtois won the Golden Glove that tournament.

category_label: NOT a player in Germany's starting XI for the 2014 World Cup final
explanation: Götze came off the bench in the second half and scored the winning goal in extra time.

category_label: NOT a Spanish player who started the 2010 World Cup final
explanation: Torres came on as a second-half substitute.

category_label: NOT a player who won the World Cup with Argentina in 2022
explanation: Aguero played in 3 WCs for Argentina but retired in 2021, before the 2022 title win.

category_label: NOT a country that has won a World Cup since 2000
explanation: Croatia reached the 2018 final and the 2022 semi-final but has never won.

category_label: NOT a country in the South American (CONMEBOL) qualifying region
explanation: Mexico qualifies through CONCACAF (North and Central America).

category_label: NOT a player who has played for Real Madrid AND won a World Cup
explanation: Modrić has won 6 Champions Leagues with Real Madrid but lost the 2018 WC final with Croatia.

category_label: NOT a country that has hosted the World Cup in the 21st century
explanation: The US co-hosts in 2026; its previous solo hosting was 1994.

category_label: NOT a French player who has scored a goal at a World Cup
explanation: Evra played at the 2006, 2010, and 2014 WCs for France but never scored.

category_label: NOT a defender
explanation: Iniesta is a central midfielder.

category_label: NOT an English player who played at the 2022 World Cup
explanation: Rooney retired from international football in 2017.

category_label: NOT a country that has lost a World Cup final since 1990
explanation: England's only WC final remains 1966 (won as hosts); they haven't been in a final since.

category_label: NOT a player who has scored a hat-trick at a World Cup
explanation: Despite his record WC appearances, Messi has never scored 3 goals in a single WC match.

category_label: NOT a player who won the World Cup with Germany in 2014
explanation: Reus suffered an ankle injury in a pre-tournament friendly and missed the entire 2014 WC.

category_label: NOT a country that competes in UEFA (European) qualifying
explanation: Brazil qualifies through CONMEBOL (South America).

category_label: NOT a player who scored at the 2022 World Cup
explanation: Lukaku missed several big chances for Belgium and did not score at the tournament.

category_label: NOT a Brazilian player who has won a World Cup since 1990
explanation: Casemiro has captained Brazil at recent WCs but Brazil's last title was 2002.

category_label: NOT a player who scored in the 2022 World Cup final
explanation: Giroud started the final for France but was substituted off in the 41st minute without scoring.

category_label: NOT a country that has reached a World Cup quarter-final this century
explanation: Cameroon's only WC quarter-final remains 1990; they have not reached the knockout stage since.

category_label: NOT a player who won the World Cup with Brazil in 1994
explanation: Zico had retired from international football by 1994.

category_label: NOT a goalkeeper who has won the World Cup
explanation: Kahn was Player of the Tournament in 2002 but Germany lost the final to Brazil.

category_label: NOT a player who has won the World Cup Golden Glove since 2010
explanation: Howard set the WC single-match saves record in 2014 but Neuer won the Golden Glove that tournament.

category_label: NOT a country that has lost to South Korea at a World Cup
explanation: Argentina beat South Korea 4-1 in the 2010 group stage; the teams haven't met in a knockout.

category_label: NOT a player who has scored in a World Cup semi-final
explanation: Belgium lost the 2018 SF 1-0 to France; Hazard played but did not score, his only WC semi-final appearance.

category_label: NOT a manager who has won the World Cup since 1990
explanation: Bielsa managed Argentina at the 2002 WC and Chile at 2010 but never lifted the trophy.

category_label: NOT a country that has won the World Cup multiple times
explanation: Spain's only World Cup title is 2010.

category_label: NOT a player who scored for Argentina at the 2022 World Cup
explanation: Lautaro played every match but Julián Álvarez took the starting striker spot and Lautaro did not score in the tournament.

category_label: NOT a player who played in the 2014 World Cup final for Argentina
explanation: Di María injured his thigh in the QF against Belgium and missed the SF and the final.

category_label: NOT a country in CONCACAF (North and Central American) qualifying
explanation: Ecuador qualifies through CONMEBOL (South America).

category_label: NOT a country that has finished as World Cup runners-up
explanation: Belgium's best WC finish is third place at 2018.

category_label: NOT a manager who has led a national team to a World Cup final
explanation: Ferguson took Scotland to the 1986 WC as caretaker after Jock Stein's death; eliminated in the group stage, he never reached a final.

category_label: NOT a player who has won the UEFA Champions League AND a World Cup
explanation: Salah won the 2019 Champions League with Liverpool but Egypt has not won a World Cup.

category_label: NOT a country that has beaten Germany at a World Cup since 2014
explanation: Saudi Arabia beat Argentina at the 2022 WC but has not faced Germany at a recent tournament.

category_label: NOT a player from Spain's 2010 World Cup winning squad
explanation: Raúl was famously left out of Spain's golden generation and was not in the 2010 squad.

category_label: NOT a country that has hosted a World Cup since 1990
explanation: China has bid for the World Cup multiple times but has never hosted.

category_label: NOT a country in the Asian (AFC) qualifying region
explanation: Egypt qualifies through CAF (Africa).

category_label: NOT a player who scored at the 2018 World Cup
explanation: Özil played as Germany were eliminated in the group stage and did not score at the tournament.

category_label: NOT a goalkeeper who started a World Cup final this century
explanation: Čech was a top European keeper but the Czech Republic never reached a World Cup final.

category_label: NOT a Manchester City player who has played at a World Cup
explanation: Algeria did not qualify for a World Cup during Mahrez's career at Manchester City.

category_label: NOT a player who scored at the 2010 World Cup
explanation: Torres was carrying an injury and did not score at the tournament Spain won.

category_label: NOT a player from Italy's 1994 World Cup squad
explanation: Pirlo was 15 in 1994 and made his Italy debut in 2002; his first WC was 2002.

category_label: NOT a country that has finished in the top 4 at a World Cup since 2014
explanation: Uruguay reached the R16 in 2014, the QF in 2018, and exited the group in 2022; never top 4 in this window.

category_label: NOT a player who scored against Brazil at the 2014 World Cup
explanation: Higuaín scored at 2014 (against Belgium in the QF) but Argentina and Brazil did not meet at the tournament.

category_label: NOT a player who has won the Best Young Player award at a World Cup
explanation: Pedri played at 2022 but Enzo Fernández won the Best Young Player.

category_label: NOT a player who scored in a World Cup final since 2000
explanation: Villa was Spain's top scorer at the 2010 tournament with 5 goals but did not score in the final itself.

category_label: NOT a player who has appeared in 2 or more World Cup finals
explanation: England did not reach a World Cup final during Kane's career.

category_label: NOT a country that has lost to Croatia at a World Cup
explanation: France beat Croatia in the 2018 final and the teams have not met in a knockout since.

category_label: NOT a player who has scored at a World Cup for Germany
explanation: Kimmich played at the 2018 and 2022 WCs but Germany were eliminated in the group both times without him scoring.

category_label: NOT a country that has lost a World Cup match on penalties since 2000
explanation: Belgium has not been to a WC penalty shootout this century; their knockout exits in 2014 and 2018 came in regulation.

category_label: NOT a player who has scored at multiple World Cups
explanation: Götze's only World Cup appearance was 2014, where he scored the final's winning goal.

category_label: NOT a country that has reached a World Cup semi-final since 2010
explanation: Senegal reached the QF in 2002 but their more recent WC exits (2018 R16, 2022 R16) have been before the semi.

category_label: NOT a player from Brazil's starting XI in the 2014 World Cup semi-final loss to Germany
explanation: Thiago Silva was suspended for the SF after picking up his second yellow in the QF against Colombia.

category_label: NOT a player who has scored more World Cup goals than Pelé
explanation: Maradona scored 8 WC goals across 4 tournaments; Pelé scored 12.

category_label: NOT a country that has played in a World Cup final
explanation: Portugal's best WC finish is third place in 1966; never reached a final.

category_label: NOT a player who scored at the 2014 World Cup
explanation: Hazard played every match as Belgium reached the QF but did not score.

category_label: NOT a player who won the World Cup with France in 1998 or 2018
explanation: Platini retired from international football in 1987, before France's 1990 WC failure.

category_label: NOT a country that has scored in a World Cup final since 2000
explanation: The Netherlands lost the 2010 final 1-0 to Spain in extra time without scoring.

category_label: NOT a player who has appeared at every World Cup since 2010
explanation: Neymar's first World Cup was 2014; he was not in the 2010 squad.

category_label: NOT a player who has scored 5 or more goals at a single World Cup
explanation: Iniesta's biggest single-WC haul was 2 goals at 2010, including the final winner.

category_label: NOT a player who has won the World Cup with Italy
explanation: Baggio reached the 1994 final but Italy lost on penalties to Brazil; he never won.

category_label: NOT a city in Qatar that hosted 2022 World Cup matches
explanation: Abu Dhabi is in the UAE; all 2022 venues were in Qatar.

category_label: NOT a FIFA World Player of the Year winner who has played at a World Cup
explanation: Weah won the award in 1995 but Liberia never qualified for a World Cup.

category_label: NOT a country that has won both the Copa América and a World Cup
explanation: Chile won Copa América in 2015 and 2016 but has never won a World Cup.

category_label: NOT a country whose first World Cup appearance was in this century
explanation: Italy's first World Cup was 1934.

category_label: NOT a country that has hosted a World Cup more than once
explanation: Russia hosted only the 2018 World Cup.

category_label: NOT a country that has co-hosted a World Cup
explanation: South Africa hosted alone in 2010; Japan-Korea co-hosted 2002 and Canada co-hosts 2026.

category_label: NOT a forward
explanation: Bellingham plays as an attacking midfielder.

category_label: NOT a player who has scored in a World Cup final
explanation: England did not reach a World Cup final during Beckham's career.

category_label: NOT a player who has scored 6 or more World Cup goals in his career
explanation: Ramos has scored just 1 WC goal (a penalty against Russia in the 2018 round of 16) across 4 tournaments.

category_label: NOT a country that has reached a World Cup semi-final since 2014
explanation: Russia's best WC finish since 2014 is the quarter-final at the 2018 tournament they hosted.

category_label: NOT a country that competed at the 2022 World Cup
explanation: Colombia finished 6th in CONMEBOL qualifying and missed the tournament.

category_label: NOT a player who has scored from a direct free kick at a World Cup since 2000
explanation: Kane's WC goals are penalties, tap-ins, and headers; he has not scored a direct free kick at a tournament.

category_label: NOT a country that has finished 4th at a World Cup this century
explanation: Senegal's best WC finishes are the 2002 quarter-final and the 2018 and 2022 round of 16; never 4th.

category_label: NOT a country that won its opening match at the 2022 World Cup
explanation: Germany lost their opener 1-2 to Japan.

category_label: NOT a player who has scored against Argentina at a World Cup since 2010
explanation: Senegal and Argentina have not met at a World Cup during Mané's international career.

category_label: NOT a country that has produced a World Cup Golden Boot winner since 2000
explanation: Mbappé won the 2022 Golden Boot with 8 goals; Messi's 7 finished second.

category_label: NOT a person who has appeared at a World Cup as both a player and a manager
explanation: Parreira was a fitness coach for Brazil in 1970 and managed at 6 World Cups, but he never played at one.

category_label: NOT a player who has won the World Cup Golden Boot since 1990
explanation: Romário won the Golden Ball at 1994 but Stoichkov and Salenko tied for the Golden Boot with 6 goals; Romário scored 5.
